mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals consumer demand strength. Sustained increases in RSAFS raise corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the directional accuracy of RSAFS month-over-month growth leading to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 55% over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expansion reflects real economic activity. Persistent month-over-month increases in INDPRO lead to higher corporate profits and lift S&P 500 returns over a quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is below 0.10 over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals economic weakening that erodes earnings expectations. Such a crossing event predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, after UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth turns negative (RSAFS month-over-month < 0) while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 5%, the combination of weakening demand and restrictive monetary policy strongly signals negative earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (RSAFS decline + FEDFUNDS above 5%) has less than 55% directional accuracy (negative returns) over any 3-year period, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime (defined by positive GDP growth and falling unemployment), industrial production (INDPRO) growth is a powerful predictor of equity returns as capacity utilization expands. Higher INDPRO leads to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0.5% and UNRATE month-over-month change < 0.1%
failure_condition: If, in the defined regime, the accuracy of INDPRO positive growth predicting positive 60-day returns is below 60% over the next 10 occurrences, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% signals imminent economic contraction. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as market participants adjust expectations downward.
failure_condition: If, after a HOUST monthly decline exceeding 10%, the 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm a broad economic contraction. This joint deterioration strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0 and INDPRO month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: If the joint negative signal (both PAYEMS and INDPRO declining) has less than 60% directional accuracy (negative 20-day returns) over any 20 occurrences, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a sustained high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3%), a high federal funds rate indicates aggressive monetary tightening that suppresses equity valuations. This relationship predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%), the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
